models.Goal: A single long-term objective belonging to a specific time horizon (1-year, 5-year, or 10-year). Has a required title and an optional description. Goals help users connect their daily work to bigger ambitions.
models.Horizon: A time-based grouping for goals. There are exactly three fixed horizons: 1-year, 5-year, and 10-year.

relationships: Each Goal belongs to exactly one Horizon
A Horizon can have zero or more Goals

goals.title: Ship the TaskHub MVP and get 100 paying users
goals.description: Build, launch, and iterate until we hit the first meaningful revenue milestone.

goals.title: Establish a consistent daily writing habit
goals.description: Write for at least 30 minutes every morning before opening email or Slack.

goals.title: Complete a half marathon
goals.description: Train progressively over 6 months and run the October local race.

goals.title: Pay off the remaining credit card balance
goals.description: Eliminate the last $4,200 using the debt avalanche method while keeping monthly savings consistent.

goals.label: 5 Years
goals.title: Grow TaskHub to $1M ARR
goals.description: Build a small, profitable team and a product that genuinely helps thousands of people manage their lives.

goals.title: Buy a home
goals.description: Save enough for a down payment on a modest home in a walkable neighborhood close to good schools.

goals.title: Become fluent in Spanish
goals.description: Be able to hold a full conversation, read literature, and travel comfortably in Spanish-speaking countries.

goals.title: Achieve financial independence
goals.description: Reach a point where passive income covers living expenses and work becomes a choice rather than a necessity.

goals.title: Write and publish a book
goals.description: Write something that matters — a non-fiction book on productivity, systems, or intentional living. Traditional or self-published.

goals.title: Mentor 10 early-career founders
goals.description: Give back by being the advisor I wish I'd had — honest, available, and genuinely invested in their success.
